content.problemStatement:
Chef has a list of jobs, each with a start time jobStartTimesjobStartTimesjobStartTimes, an end time jobEndTimesjobEndTimesjobEndTimes, and a profit associated with completing it jobProfitsjobProfitsjobProfits. **Chef wants to schedule these jobs in such a way that no two jobs overlap in their time intervals**. 

If Chef completes a job that ends at time XXX, Chef can start another job that begins exactly at time XXX. 

Help Chef find the maximum total profit Chef can earn by selecting a subset of non-overlapping jobs.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecalculateMaxProfitcalculateMaxProfitcalculateMaxProfit - This function computes the maximum profit achievable by scheduling non-overlapping jobs based on their start times, end times, and associated profits.

Parameters
jobStartTimesjobStartTimesjobStartTimes: A list of integers representing the start times of each job.
jobEndTimesjobEndTimesjobEndTimes : A list of integers representing the end times of each job.
jobProfitsjobProfitsjobProfits : A list of integers representing the profit earned from each job.

All vectors have the same length, representing the total number of jobs.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the maximum total profit from a subset of non-overlapping jobs.
`The input and output formats given below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.`

Constraints
1â‰¤Tâ‰¤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51â‰¤Tâ‰¤105
1â‰¤numberOfJobsâ‰¤5Ă—1041 \leq number Of Jobs \leq 5 \times 10^41â‰¤numberOfJobsâ‰¤5Ă—104
1â‰¤jobStartTimes[i]<jobEndTimes[i]â‰¤1091 \leq jobStartTimes[i] < jobEndTimes[i] \leq 10^91â‰¤jobStartTimes[i]<jobEndTimes[i]â‰¤109
1â‰¤jobProfits[i]â‰¤1041 \leq jobProfits[i] \leq 10^41â‰¤jobProfits[i]â‰¤104

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT â€” the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of 3 lines:

A line with comma separated integers â€” representing Chef's jobStartTimesjobStartTimesjobStartTimes.
A line with comma separated integers â€” representing Chef's jobEndTimesjobEndTimesjobEndTimes.
A line with comma separated integers â€” representing Chef's jobProfitsjobProfitsjobProfits.




Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing the maximum total profit Chef can achieve by selecting a subset of non-overlapping jobs.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the single test case, selecting jobs (1 to 3), (3 to 5), and (6 to 9) yields profits 20 + 50 + 70 = 140.

Explanation:
For the single test case, selecting jobs with intervals [1-3], [4-7], and [7-10] yields profits 10 + 30 + 50 = 90.

Explanation:
For the single test case, we can only choose jobs (4 to 8) gives profits 12, which is maximum without overlapping.
